ZEAL/BZea NBR — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

R/qtl interval mapping (Haley–Knott) for NBR on the BC2S3 RTIGER ancestry mosaic (B73=A / het=H / teosinte=B). Marker set + genetic map = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM set (9,063 markers, TeoNAM cM — NOT computed from ZEAL); the ZEAL mosaic is projected onto those positions. Significance = genome-wide 1000-permutation LOD threshold (a single shared permutation null across all traits). Candidates = the NBR candidate-gene panel (B73 v5).

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 13, 2026

What this is. The NBR R/qtl scan — a sibling of the DTA/DTS/PH notebooks. All ZEAL traits go through one joint run (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R): a single bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, one calc.genoprob, one Haley–Knott scanone, and one shared 1000-permutation null across the whole panel; this notebook reads the pre-computed zeal_nbr_* slices. Multi-peak detection and per-family scans as in the other R/qtl notebooks.

ImportantMarker set, map, and genotype — read this first
  • No ZEAL genetic map is estimated (excess heterozygotes make a ZEAL est.map unreliable), so every cM is a TeoNAM distance; the marker set + map is the TeoNAM JLM grid, and the ZEAL RTIGER mosaic is projected onto it (nearest block). Markers are named S<chr>_<pos_v5>.
  • Genotypes are HMM-error-corrected before the scan (calc.genoprob(error.prob=0.01)); the per-taxon additive scan uses the HMM expected dosage. Peak CIs are the 1.5-LOD support on the real markers (matching R/qtl lodint).
Show code 
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "NBR"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code 
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")
Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.
metric value
individuals (NBR) 1380
markers (TeoNAM JLM grid) 9063
chromosomes 10
H / het 0.090
B / teosinte 0.020

No covariate vs taxon covariate

Show code 
peakrow <- function(tag) fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
cmp <- data.table(
  model = c("no covariate", "+ taxon (5-family)"),
  `perm 5% LOD` = round(c(nc$thr5, tx$thr5), 2),
  `perm 10% LOD` = round(c(nc$thr10, tx$thr10), 2),
  `max LOD` = round(c(max(nc$one$lod), max(tx$one$lod)), 2),
  `n QTL (FWER 0.05)` = c(nrow(peakrow("nocovar")), nrow(peakrow("taxon")))
)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "No covariate vs the 5-family taxon covariate: permutation thresholds, max LOD, and one-per-chromosome QTL counts.")
No covariate vs the 5-family taxon covariate: permutation thresholds, max LOD, and one-per-chromosome QTL counts.
model perm 5% LOD perm 10% LOD max LOD n QTL (FWER 0.05)
no covariate 4.52 4.14 6.48 1
+ taxon (5-family) 4.49 4.11 6.47 1

Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)

Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the candidate genes (v5, on the cM axis).

Show code 
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = candidate genes", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each candidate (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_genes.csv × candidate_evidence.csv). These are the genes marked on the LOD profile above; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

Show code 
source(here::here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R"))
candidate_table(TRAIT)
Candidate genes for NBR from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_*.csv): 15 loci (14 mapped to B73 v5, 1 unmapped). DOI links where curated; “TODO” = evidence not yet curated.
gene full name locus tier direction evidence phenotype / note reference
rtcs1 rootless concerning crown and seminal roots1 1:10.9 Mb 2 loss LOB-domain (LBD) protein; master regulator of embryonic seminal & post-embryonic shoot-borne (crown+brace) root initiation; rtcs lacks shoot-borne roots 10.1111/j.1365-313X.2007.03075.x
vp8 viviparous8 1:293.1 Mb 2 increase vp8 mutant INCREASES number of brace-root whorls (also affects leaf/embryo development) 10.1104/pp.108.116202
ZmYUC4 YUCCA4 (auxin biosynthesis) 2:113.8 Mb 1 angle Flavin monooxygenase, auxin biosynthesis; Zmyuc4 single & Zmyuc2/4 double mutant enlarge brace root angle 10.1111/nph.18733
ZmARF11 auxin response factor 11 3:201.7 Mb 1 increase ARF downstream of miR390-TAS3; STTM390 up-regulates ZmARF11 → INCREASED brace root length & whorl number 10.3390/ijms25126791
rum1 rootless with undetectable meristem 1 3:214.7 Mb 3 loss Monocot-specific Aux/IAA (ZmIAA10); controls embryonic seminal & post-embryonic lateral root initiation 10.1111/j.1365-313X.2011.04495.x
bige1 big embryo1 5:1.9 Mb 2 increase trans-Golgi MATE transporter; bige1 mutant INCREASES number of brace-root whorls / nodes with roots (accelerated organ initiation) 10.1073/pnas.1411851112
argos8 auxin-regulated gene involved in organ size 8 6:154.0 Mb 2 decrease Negative regulator of ethylene signalling; blocks nodal/brace root emergence (not primordia formation) → REDUCED number of nodal roots 10.1111/pbi.13077
ZmRAP2.7 related to AP2.7 / vegetative to generative transition1 (vgt1) 8:135.7 Mb 1 decrease AP2/ERF TF, miR172 target; RAP2.7-Mu mutant DECREASES brace root number (crown roots unaffected); Corngrass1 (elevated) INCREASES brace roots 10.3389/fpls.2019.00820
rul1 rum1-like1 8:154.5 Mb 3 modifier Homeolog of RUM1 (Aux/IAA); conserved/unique features in auxin-signalling root initiation 10.1104/pp.15.00107
lrp1 lateral root primordia 1 8:163.9 Mb 3 modifier Transcriptional activator directly regulated by RUM1 (Aux/IAA); auxin-inducible, confined to early root primordia/meristems 10.1093/jxb/erv187
ZmARF26 auxin response factor 26 8:180.7 Mb 1 increase ARF downstream of miR390-TAS3; up-regulated in STTM390; loss-of-function alters brace root growth 10.3390/ijms25126791
rtcl1 rtcs-like1 9:158.9 Mb 2 decrease LBD paralog of RTCS; reduced elongation of shoot-borne crown roots; cooperative with RTCS downstream of ARF 10.1111/nph.13420
ZmYUC2 YUCCA2 (auxin biosynthesis) 10:100.9 Mb 1 angle Flavin monooxygenase, auxin biosynthesis; Zmyuc2/4 double mutant enlarges brace root angle & raises lodging resistance 10.1111/nph.18733
lrt1 lateral rootless1 10:151.5 Mb 3 loss DCAF subunit of CUL4 E3 ligase; lrt1 defective in LATERAL roots of primary/seminal roots but NOT shoot-borne roots (cloned via BSA-seq+CRISPR) 10.1111/nph.18599
rt1 rootless1 unmapped (not plotted) 2 loss Classic rootless1 mutant; strongly reduced formation of nodal/brace (shoot-borne) roots (locus, gene not molecularly cloned) Jenkins 1930 (classical locus)

Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05) — one per chromosome, with candidate colocalization

Show code 
cipk <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_ci_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(cipk) == 0) {
  cat("No genome-wide significant peaks.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    cipk[order(chr, -lod), .(
      chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), pval, marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = "Genome-wide significant QTL (taxon model), 1.5-LOD CI in v5 Mb, with any candidate gene inside."
  )
}
Genome-wide significant QTL (taxon model), 1.5-LOD CI in v5 Mb, with any candidate gene inside.
chr pos (cM) pos (Mb) LOD pval marker 1.5-LOD CI (Mb) candidate in CI
1 109.2 222.1 6.47 0.002994 S1_222106648 205.9–222.7 –

Multi-peak refinement (airmine refine_peaks)

Show code 
refined <- function(tag) {
  r <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  if (nrow(r) == 0) {
    return(r)
  }
  r[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thresh, 2), marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )]
}
rt <- refined("taxon")
if (nrow(rt) == 0) cat("No refined peaks.") else knitr::kable(rt, caption = sprintf("Refined multi-peak QTL, taxon model (%d QTL across %d chromosomes).", nrow(rt), uniqueN(rt$chr)))
Refined multi-peak QTL, taxon model (3 QTL across 1 chromosomes).
chr pos (cM) pos (Mb) LOD perm 5% thr marker 1.5-LOD CI (Mb) candidate in CI
1 109.2 222.1 6.47 4.49 S1_222106648 221.0–222.7 –
1 99.2 205.9 5.81 4.49 S1_205851349 195.7–207.5 –
1 129.8 264.9 4.53 4.49 S1_264927314 243.3–294.6 vp8

Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC

Show code 
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = candidate genes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt
Show code 
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]
knitr::kable(btpk, caption = "Genome-wide additive peak per taxon, with the per-taxon 1000-perm 5% threshold and any candidate gene in the CI. 'sig' = the family's peak exceeds its own threshold.")
Genome-wide additive peak per taxon, with the per-taxon 1000-perm 5% threshold and any candidate gene in the CI. ‘sig’ = the family’s peak exceeds its own threshold.
taxon chr pos (cM) pos (Mb) marker max LOD perm 5% thr sig 1.5-LOD CI (Mb) candidate in CI
Zd 1 124.6 259.7 S1_259650635 3.20 3.40 no 201.4–296.6 vp8
Zv 6 125.3 180.6 S6_180647162 2.85 3.64 no 133.9–180.6 argos8
Zx 1 99.2 205.9 S1_205851349 2.52 3.41 no 24.7–302.0 vp8
Zl 9 79.1 125.6 S9_125579331 2.50 3.41 no 97.1–147.6 –
Zh 4 108.6 207.9 S4_207888359 1.88 3.83 no 7.6–221.0 –

Per-taxon multi-peak refinement

Show code 
btr <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(btr) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon refined peaks cleared the per-family thresholds.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    btr[order(taxon, chr, -lod), .(
      taxon, chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5, 2), marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = sprintf("Refined per-taxon multi-peak QTL (%d QTL across %d families).", nrow(btr), uniqueN(btr$taxon))
  )
}
No per-taxon refined peaks cleared the per-family thresholds.

Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon

Show code 
if (nrow(btpk[sig == "yes"]) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.")
} else {
  top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
  mk <- top$marker
  gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
  yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
  ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
  ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
  d <- data.table(
    taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
    geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
  )[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
  summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
  shown <- summ[n >= 3]
  ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
  ncand_txt <- if (nrow(ncand)) sprintf("; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb", ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6) else ""
  ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
    geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
    geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb%s)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand_txt),
      y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
      title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
      subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  print(ef)
}
No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.

Read. Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

Source Code
---
title: "ZEAL/BZea NBR — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects"
subtitle: "R/qtl interval mapping (Haley–Knott) for NBR on the BC2S3 RTIGER ancestry mosaic (B73=A / het=H / teosinte=B). Marker set + genetic map = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM set (9,063 markers, TeoNAM cM — NOT computed from ZEAL); the ZEAL mosaic is projected onto those positions. Significance = genome-wide 1000-permutation LOD threshold (a single shared permutation null across all traits). Candidates = the NBR candidate-gene panel (B73 v5)."
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

**What this is.** The NBR R/qtl scan — a sibling of the DTA/DTS/PH notebooks. All ZEAL traits go
through one joint run (`scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R`): a single `bcsft` (BC₂S₃) cross, one
`calc.genoprob`, one Haley–Knott `scanone`, and **one shared 1000-permutation null** across the whole
panel; this notebook reads the pre-computed `zeal_nbr_*` slices. Multi-peak detection and
per-family scans as in the other R/qtl notebooks.

::: {.callout-important}
## Marker set, map, and genotype — read this first
- **No ZEAL genetic map is estimated** (excess heterozygotes make a ZEAL `est.map` unreliable), so
  **every cM is a TeoNAM distance**; the marker set + map is the TeoNAM JLM grid, and the ZEAL
  RTIGER mosaic is **projected** onto it (nearest block). Markers are named `S<chr>_<pos_v5>`.
- Genotypes are **HMM-error-corrected before the scan** (`calc.genoprob(error.prob=0.01)`); the
  per-taxon additive scan uses the HMM **expected dosage**. Peak CIs are the 1.5-LOD support on the
  real markers (matching R/qtl `lodint`).
:::

```{r setup}
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "NBR"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
```

```{r meta}
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")
```

## No covariate vs taxon covariate

```{r compare-table}
peakrow <- function(tag) fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
cmp <- data.table(
  model = c("no covariate", "+ taxon (5-family)"),
  `perm 5% LOD` = round(c(nc$thr5, tx$thr5), 2),
  `perm 10% LOD` = round(c(nc$thr10, tx$thr10), 2),
  `max LOD` = round(c(max(nc$one$lod), max(tx$one$lod)), 2),
  `n QTL (FWER 0.05)` = c(nrow(peakrow("nocovar")), nrow(peakrow("taxon")))
)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "No covariate vs the 5-family taxon covariate: permutation thresholds, max LOD, and one-per-chromosome QTL counts.")
```

## Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)

Faceted by chromosome. **Blue** = scanone + taxon covariate; **grey** = no covariate; dashed = the
1000-permutation 5% thresholds. **Dotted grey verticals** mark the candidate genes (v5, on the cM axis).

```{r lod-profile, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.6}
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = candidate genes", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p
```

## Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each **candidate** (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (`reference/candidate_genes.csv` × `candidate_evidence.csv`). These are the genes marked on the LOD profile above; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

```{r candidate-registry}
source(here::here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R"))
candidate_table(TRAIT)
```

## Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05) — one per chromosome, with candidate colocalization

```{r peaks}
cipk <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_ci_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(cipk) == 0) {
  cat("No genome-wide significant peaks.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    cipk[order(chr, -lod), .(
      chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), pval, marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = "Genome-wide significant QTL (taxon model), 1.5-LOD CI in v5 Mb, with any candidate gene inside."
  )
}
```

### Multi-peak refinement (airmine `refine_peaks`)

```{r refined-peaks}
refined <- function(tag) {
  r <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  if (nrow(r) == 0) {
    return(r)
  }
  r[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thresh, 2), marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )]
}
rt <- refined("taxon")
if (nrow(rt) == 0) cat("No refined peaks.") else knitr::kable(rt, caption = sprintf("Refined multi-peak QTL, taxon model (%d QTL across %d chromosomes).", nrow(rt), uniqueN(rt$chr)))
```

## Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC

```{r per-taxon, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.6}
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = candidate genes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt
```

```{r per-taxon-peaks}
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]
knitr::kable(btpk, caption = "Genome-wide additive peak per taxon, with the per-taxon 1000-perm 5% threshold and any candidate gene in the CI. 'sig' = the family's peak exceeds its own threshold.")
```

### Per-taxon multi-peak refinement

```{r per-taxon-refined}
btr <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(btr) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon refined peaks cleared the per-family thresholds.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    btr[order(taxon, chr, -lod), .(
      taxon, chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5, 2), marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = sprintf("Refined per-taxon multi-peak QTL (%d QTL across %d families).", nrow(btr), uniqueN(btr$taxon))
  )
}
```

## Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon

```{r effect, fig.width=10, fig.height=4.2}
if (nrow(btpk[sig == "yes"]) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.")
} else {
  top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
  mk <- top$marker
  gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
  yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
  ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
  ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
  d <- data.table(
    taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
    geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
  )[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
  summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
  shown <- summ[n >= 3]
  ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
  ncand_txt <- if (nrow(ncand)) sprintf("; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb", ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6) else ""
  ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
    geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
    geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb%s)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand_txt),
      y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
      title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
      subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  print(ef)
}
```

**Read.** Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. `A/A` = B73, `A/B` = het,
`B/B` = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a candidate (the "candidate in CI" columns) is
suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.
